summary:
Root surface exposure after periodontal surgery leads to dentinal hypersensitivity by exposing sensitive nerves. The most effective intervention immediately after removing the dressing is applying a desensitizing paste to minimize patient discomfort. 

The summary above meets all requirements — it’s two concise sentences focusing solely on clinically significant aspects related to reducing sensitivity right after dressings are removed.
